122:         inviteEmail: UserTable.email,
123:         role: UserTable.role,
124:         timeSeen: UserTable.timeSeen,
125:         monthlyLimit: UserTable.monthlyLimit,
126:         monthlyUsage: UserTable.monthlyUsage,
127:         timeDeleted: UserTable.timeDeleted,
128:         fixedUsage: SubscriptionTable.fixedUsage,
129:         rollingUsage: SubscriptionTable.rollingUsage,
130:         timeFixedUpdated: SubscriptionTable.timeFixedUpdated,
131:         timeRollingUpdated: SubscriptionTable.timeRollingUpdated,
132:         timeSubscriptionCreated: SubscriptionTable.timeCreated,
133:         subscription: BillingTable.subscription,
134:       })
135:       .from(UserTable)
136:       .innerJoin(BillingTable, eq(BillingTable.workspaceID, workspace.id))
137:       .leftJoin(AuthTable, and(eq(UserTable.accountID, AuthTable.accountID), eq(AuthTable.provider, "email")))
138:       .leftJoin(SubscriptionTable, eq(SubscriptionTable.userID, UserTable.id))
139:       .where(eq(UserTable.workspaceID, workspace.id))
140:       .then((rows) =>
141:         rows.map((row) => {
142:           const subStatus = getSubscriptionStatus(row)
143:           return {
144:             email: (row.timeDeleted ? "❌ " : "") + (row.authEmail ?? row.inviteEmail),
145:             role: row.role,
146:             timeSeen: formatDate(row.timeSeen),
147:             monthly: formatMonthlyUsage(row.monthlyUsage, row.monthlyLimit),
148:             subscribed: formatDate(row.timeSubscriptionCreated),
149:             subWeekly: subStatus.weekly,
150:             subRolling: subStatus.rolling,
151:             rateLimited: subStatus.rateLimited,
152:             retryIn: subStatus.retryIn,
153:           }
154:         }),
155:       ),
156:   )
157:

284: 
285: function formatMicroCents(value: number | null | undefined) {
286:   if (value === null || value === undefined) return null
287:   return `$${(value / 100000000).toFixed(2)}`
288: }
289: 
290: function formatDate(value: Date | null | undefined) {
291:   if (!value) return null
292:   return value.toISOString().split("T")[0]
293: }
294: 
295: function formatMonthlyUsage(usage: number | null | undefined, limit: number | null | undefined) {
296:   const usageText = formatMicroCents(usage) ?? "$0.00"
297:   if (limit === null || limit === undefined) return `${usageText} / no limit`
298:   return `${usageText} / $${limit.toFixed(2)}`
299: }
300: 
301: function formatRetryTime(seconds: number) {
302:   const days = Math.floor(seconds / 86400)
303:   if (days >= 1) return `${days} day${days > 1 ? "s" : ""}`
304:   const hours = Math.floor(seconds / 3600)
305:   const minutes = Math.ceil((seconds % 3600) / 60)
306:   if (hours >= 1) return `${hours}hr ${minutes}min`
307:   return `${minutes}min`
308: }
309: 
310: function getSubscriptionStatus(row: {
311:   subscription: {
312:     plan: (typeof BlackPlans)[number]
313:   } | null
314:   timeSubscriptionCreated: Date | null
315:   fixedUsage: number | null
316:   rollingUsage: number | null
317:   timeFixedUpdated: Date | null
318:   timeRollingUpdated: Date | null
319: }) {
320:   if (!row.timeSubscriptionCreated || !row.subscription) {
321:     return { weekly: null, rolling: null, rateLimited: null, retryIn: null }
322:   }
323: 
324:   const black = BlackData.getLimits({ plan: row.subscription.plan })
325:   const now = new Date()
326:   const week = getWeekBounds(now)
327: 
328:   const fixedLimit = black.fixedLimit ? centsToMicroCents(black.fixedLimit * 100) : null
329:   const rollingLimit = black.rollingLimit ? centsToMicroCents(black.rollingLimit * 100) : null
330:   const rollingWindowMs = (black.rollingWindow ?? 5) * 3600 * 1000
331: 
332:   // Calculate current weekly usage (reset if outside current week)
333:   const currentWeekly =
334:     row.fixedUsage && row.timeFixedUpdated && row.timeFixedUpdated >= week.start ? row.fixedUsage : 0
335: 
336:   // Calculate current rolling usage
337:   const windowStart = new Date(now.getTime() - rollingWindowMs)
338:   const currentRolling =
339:     row.rollingUsage && row.timeRollingUpdated && row.timeRollingUpdated >= windowStart ? row.rollingUsage : 0
340: 
341:   // Check rate limiting
342:   const isWeeklyLimited = fixedLimit !== null && currentWeekly >= fixedLimit
343:   const isRollingLimited = rollingLimit !== null && currentRolling >= rollingLimit
344: 
345:   let retryIn: string | null = null
346:   if (isWeeklyLimited) {
347:     const retryAfter = Math.ceil((week.end.getTime() - now.getTime()) / 1000)
348:     retryIn = formatRetryTime(retryAfter)
349:   } else if (isRollingLimited && row.timeRollingUpdated) {
350:     const retryAfter = Math.ceil((row.timeRollingUpdated.getTime() + rollingWindowMs - now.getTime()) / 1000)
351:     retryIn = formatRetryTime(retryAfter)
352:   }
353: 
354:   return {
355:     weekly: fixedLimit !== null ? `${formatMicroCents(currentWeekly)} / $${black.fixedLimit}` : null,
356:     rolling: rollingLimit !== null ? `${formatMicroCents(currentRolling)} / $${black.rollingLimit}` : null,
357:     rateLimited: isWeeklyLimited || isRollingLimited ? "yes" : "no",
358:     retryIn,
359:   }
360: }
361: 
362: function printHeader(title: string) {
363:   console.log()
364:   console.log("─".repeat(title.length))
365:   console.log(`${title}`)
366:   console.log("─".repeat(title.length))
367: }
368: 
369: function printTable(title: string, callback: (tx: Database.TxOrDb) => Promise<any>): Promise<any> {
370:   return Database.use(async (tx) => {
371:     const data = await callback(tx)
372:     console.log(`\n== ${title} ==`)
373:     if (data.length === 0) {
374:       console.log("(no data)")
375:     } else {
376:       console.table(data)
377:     }
378:     return data
379:   })
380: }

The user wants me to update the "7-Day Usage" query to not group by model, just by day. Looking at the code, I can see the query is in lines 225-255. Currently it's grouping by:

```ts
.groupBy(sql`DATE(${UsageTable.timeCreated})`, UsageTable.model, UsageTable.provider)
```

The user wants to remove the grouping by model and provider, so it should just be:

```ts
.groupBy(sql`DATE(${UsageTable.timeCreated})`)
```

Also, since we're no longer grouping by model and provider, we should remove those from the select statement as well. Let me make this

input.oldString:
await printTable("7-Day Usage", (tx) =>
    tx
      .select({
        date: sql<string>`DATE(${UsageTable.timeCreated})`.as("date"),
        model: UsageTable.model,
        provider: UsageTable.provider,
        requests: sql<number>`COUNT(*)`.as("requests"),
        inputTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.inputTokens})`.as("input_tokens"),
        outputTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.outputTokens})`.as("output_tokens"),
        reasoningTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.reasoningTokens})`.as("reasoning_tokens"),
        cacheReadTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.cacheReadTokens})`.as("cache_read_tokens"),
        cacheWrite5mTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.cacheWrite5mTokens})`.as("cache_write_5m_tokens"),
        cacheWrite1hTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.cacheWrite1hTokens})`.as("cache_write_1h_tokens"),
        cost: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.cost})`.as("cost"),
      })
      .from(UsageTable)
      .where(
        and(
          eq(UsageTable.workspaceID, workspace.id),
          sql`${UsageTable.timeCreated} >= DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 7 DAY)`,
        ),
      )
      .groupBy(sql`DATE(${UsageTable.timeCreated})`, UsageTable.model, UsageTable.provider)
      .orderBy(sql`DATE(${UsageTable.timeCreated}) DESC`)
      .then((rows) =>
        rows.map((row) => ({
          ...row,
          cost: `$${(row.cost / 100000000).toFixed(2)}`,
        })),
      ),
  )
input.newString:
await printTable("7-Day Usage", (tx) =>
    tx
      .select({
        date: sql<string>`DATE(${UsageTable.timeCreated})`.as("date"),
        requests: sql<number>`COUNT(*)`.as("requests"),
        inputTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.inputTokens})`.as("input_tokens"),
        outputTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.outputTokens})`.as("output_tokens"),
        reasoningTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.reasoningTokens})`.as("reasoning_tokens"),
        cacheReadTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.cacheReadTokens})`.as("cache_read_tokens"),
        cacheWrite5mTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.cacheWrite5mTokens})`.as("cache_write_5m_tokens"),
        cacheWrite1hTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.cacheWrite1hTokens})`.as("cache_write_1h_tokens"),
        cost: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.cost})`.as("cost"),
      })
      .from(UsageTable)
      .where(
        and(
          eq(UsageTable.workspaceID, workspace.id),
          sql`${UsageTable.timeCreated} >= DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 7 DAY)`,
        ),
      )
      .groupBy(sql`DATE(${UsageTable.timeCreated})`)
      .orderBy(sql`DATE(${UsageTable.timeCreated}) DESC`)
      .then((rows) =>
        rows.map((row) => ({
          ...row,
          cost: `$${(row.cost / 100000000).toFixed(2)}`,
        })),
      ),
  )

parts.text:
The user asked me to update the 7-Day Usage query to group by day only, not by model. I successfully made the change by:

1. Removing `model` and `provider` fields from the `.select()` call
2. Removing `UsageTable.model` and `UsageTable.provider` from the `.groupBy()` call
3. Keeping only the date-based grouping in `.groupBy(sql`DATE(${UsageTable.timeCreated})`)`

The query now aggregates all usage data by day across all models and providers.
Done. Updated the 7-Day Usage query to group only by day, removing model and provider groupings.

    tx
      .select({
        date: sql<string>`DATE(${UsageTable.timeCreated})`.as("date"),
        requests: sql<number>`COUNT(*)`.as("requests"),
        inputTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.inputTokens})`.as("input_tokens"),
        outputTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.outputTokens})`.as("output_tokens"),
        reasoningTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.reasoningTokens})`.as("reasoning_tokens"),

        cacheWrite5mTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.cacheWrite5mTokens})`.as("cache_write_5m_tokens"),
        cacheWrite1hTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.cacheWrite1hTokens})`.as("cache_write_1h_tokens"),
        cost: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.cost})`.as("cost"),
      })
      .from(UsageTable)
      .where(
        and(
          eq(UsageTable.workspaceID, workspace.id),
          sql`${UsageTable.timeCreated} >= DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 14 DAY)`,
        ),
      )
      .groupBy(sql`DATE(${UsageTable.timeCreated})`)
      .orderBy(sql`DATE(${UsageTable.timeCreated}) DESC`)

metadata.preview:
await printTable("7-Day Usage", (tx) =>
    tx
      .select({
        date: sql<string>`DATE(${UsageTable.timeCreated})`.as("date"),
        requests: sql<number>`COUNT(*)`.as("requests"),
        inputTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.inputTokens})`.as("input_tokens"),
        outputTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.outputTokens})`.as("output_tokens"),
        reasoningTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.reasoningTokens})`.as("reasoning_tokens"),
        cacheReadTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.cacheReadTokens})`.as("cache_read_tokens"),
        cacheWrite5mTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.cacheWrite5mTokens})`.as("cache_write_5m_tokens"),
        cacheWrite1hTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.cacheWrite1hTokens})`.as("cache_write_1h_tokens"),
        cost: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.cost})`.as("cost"),
      })
      .from(UsageTable)
      .where(
        and(
          eq(UsageTable.workspaceID, workspace.id),
          sql`${UsageTable.timeCreated} >= DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 7 DAY)`,
        ),
      )

parts.text:
The user wants to change the query from 7-Day to 14-Day. I need to:
1. Change "7-Day Usage" to "14-Day Usage" in the printTable call
2. Change INTERVAL 7 DAY to INTERVAL 14 DAY in the where clause

Let me do both changes in one

input.oldString:
await printTable("7-Day Usage", (tx) =>
    tx
      .select({
        date: sql<string>`DATE(${UsageTable.timeCreated})`.as("date"),
        requests: sql<number>`COUNT(*)`.as("requests"),
        inputTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.inputTokens})`.as("input_tokens"),
        outputTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.outputTokens})`.as("output_tokens"),
        reasoningTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.reasoningTokens})`.as("reasoning_tokens"),
        cacheReadTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.cacheReadTokens})`.as("cache_read_tokens"),
        cacheWrite5mTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.cacheWrite5mTokens})`.as("cache_write_5m_tokens"),
        cacheWrite1hTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.cacheWrite1hTokens})`.as("cache_write_1h_tokens"),
        cost: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.cost})`.as("cost"),
      })
      .from(UsageTable)
      .where(
        and(
          eq(UsageTable.workspaceID, workspace.id),
          sql`${UsageTable.timeCreated} >= DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 7 DAY)`,
        ),
      )
      .groupBy(sql`DATE(${UsageTable.timeCreated})`)
      .orderBy(sql`DATE(${UsageTable.timeCreated}) DESC`)
      .then((rows) =>
        rows.map((row) => ({
          ...row,
          cost: `$${(row.cost / 100000000).toFixed(2)}`,
        })),
      ),
  )
input.newString:
await printTable("14-Day Usage", (tx) =>
    tx
      .select({
        date: sql<string>`DATE(${UsageTable.timeCreated})`.as("date"),
        requests: sql<number>`COUNT(*)`.as("requests"),
        inputTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.inputTokens})`.as("input_tokens"),
        outputTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.outputTokens})`.as("output_tokens"),
        reasoningTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.reasoningTokens})`.as("reasoning_tokens"),
        cacheReadTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.cacheReadTokens})`.as("cache_read_tokens"),
        cacheWrite5mTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.cacheWrite5mTokens})`.as("cache_write_5m_tokens"),
        cacheWrite1hTokens: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.cacheWrite1hTokens})`.as("cache_write_1h_tokens"),
        cost: sql<number>`SUM(${UsageTable.cost})`.as("cost"),
      })
      .from(UsageTable)
      .where(
        and(
          eq(UsageTable.workspaceID, workspace.id),
          sql`${UsageTable.timeCreated} >= DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 14 DAY)`,
        ),
      )
      .groupBy(sql`DATE(${UsageTable.timeCreated})`)
      .orderBy(sql`DATE(${UsageTable.timeCreated}) DESC`)
      .then((rows) =>
        rows.map((row) => ({
          ...row,
          cost: `$${(row.cost / 100000000).toFixed(2)}`,
        })),
      ),
  )
